name: Winston Lin
title: Building products, platforms, and business systems
tagline: I'm Winston. I work across serverless, platform products, and business systems. I’m increasingly using AI as part of how I think, build, and operate, and I also advise and invest in smaller businesses and startups. This site brings together case studies, writing, and Python tools from that work.
meta_description: Selected case studies, writing, and Python tools across serverless, platform products, business systems, and operator work.

oracle_blog_links.title: OCI Functions in Customer Workflows: Common Patterns and Practical Design Checklists
A Practical Guide to Cross-Region Recovery for OCI Functions
Introducing Long-Running Functions and Response Destinations with OCI Functions

intro_paragraphs: I’ve led product across serverless, CI/CD, and developer platforms, and I also work with operators and founders on growth, service design, and business systems.
experience_highlights: Principal Product Manager for OCI Functions, focused on developer experience and execution model
Previously led product work across OCI DevOps / CI/CD and adjacent platform systems
Built internal platform capabilities that shortened delivery cycles and reduced operational drag

intro: I’m based in San Francisco and work in product, mostly around cloud and developer tools. I spend a lot of time thinking about how to make products simpler and easier to use for the people building and shipping with them.

Outside of work, I invest in and advise early-stage startups in the U.S. and Asia. I like being around people building things from scratch and figuring out how something goes from an idea to something that actually works.

A lot of what I like, in product and outside of it, comes back to first-principles thinking: understanding how things work, where the real constraints are, and how businesses create value, serve customers well, and grow in ways that hold up over time.
background: I grew up between Taipei and Orange County, which gave me a mix of perspectives early on and made me comfortable moving between different environments.

I later earned a B.S. from UC Berkeley Haas, where I got more interested in the overlap between business and technology. I also spent a lot of time in tutoring, mentorship, and lab assistant roles, and stayed involved after college as a mentor in the Haas undergraduate program through 2025.
Before product, I was deeply into competitive math and community volunteering. That mix of analytical work and helping other people still shapes how I think about problems and how I like to work.
personal: Outside of work, I try to stay active and get outdoors when I can, whether that means hiking around the Bay Area, exploring new places, or just getting out of the city for a bit. I also shoot photography, mostly on Fujifilm, as a way to slow down and capture things that feel real in the moment.

I grew up playing sports and still play basketball and volleyball when I can. More recently I’ve also gotten into running and Barry’s. I like anime, especially Frieren and Your Lie in April. Lately I’ve been spending more time thinking about investing, real estate, and how people make long-term bets on places, assets, and businesses.
